title: Assume disconnect and resync on reconnect
description: Treat every connection as transient; on reconnect the client requests state since a cursor rather than trusting continuity.
body: Use when building any live feed, subscription, or collaborative view over a long-lived connection. Do not assume the socket stays up or that messages sent during a drop are delivered later. Actions: give each message a monotonic id/sequence; have the client persist the last-seen id and on reconnect send it so the server replays or snapshots from there; design idempotent client apply so a replayed message is harmless. Validation signals include a test that kills the connection mid-stream and asserts the client recovers every missed message after reconnect. Risk boundary: a reconnect path that silently loses updates leaves clients with stale state and is a correctness defect to flag.
